idea-plugin.name: Refalcon
idea-plugin.description:
Refalcon brings fast, sharp-eyed Refal-5λ language support to IntelliJ IDEA. It swoops in on your .ref and .refi files with lexer-based syntax highlighting (including string escapes and distinctly colored activation brackets) and a configurable color settings page, brace matching for { } ( ) < > [ ], line and block comments, code folding, a structure view of a file's functions, code completion (directives, built-ins, in-file functions, and in-scope variables), navigation that scales — Go to Declaration, Go to Symbol, Find Usages and Rename, including across files via $EXTERN, backed by a cached project-wide symbol index — quick documentation, an Unused function inspection, instant compiler-free error highlighting with quick-fixes (create function / add $EXTERN), a code formatter in the canonical 2-space Refal style (column-0 * comments stay pinned), fast background syntax checks via rlc --grammar-check shown inline, and a run configuration that compiles and runs programs with the rlc toolchain — or rlmake for multi-file programs (follows *$FROM) — auto-detected on the PATH and in standard install locations (on Windows, rlc.bat under %APPDATA%\Refal-5-lambda is found even when the IDE's PATH is stale). Dialect-aware: classic Refal-5 sources work as-is, and Refal-2 files are detected by content (name start headers, bare entry/extrn, k/name/ calls) and get their own lexer with highlighting, structure view, brace matching and case-insensitive go-to-definition.
Token rules were informed by the community VS Code extension (GDVFox/vscode-refal-5-lambda); the instant error highlighting is inspired by the Grammar-Kit coursework plugin bmstu-iu9/RefalFiveLambdaPlugin.
idea-plugin.change-notes: 0.0.1 — initial release. Syntax highlighting (string escapes, distinct activation brackets) and a color settings page; brace matching; line/block comments; code folding; a structure view of functions; a sentence/pattern parser; code completion for directives, built-ins, in-file functions and in-scope variables; navigation backed by a cached project-wide symbol index — Go to Declaration, Go to Symbol, Find Usages, Rename, including across files via $EXTERN; quick documentation; instant compiler-free error highlighting (Unresolved function/Unresolved variable) with quick-fixes (create function / add $EXTERN); a code formatter (canonical 2-space style, column-0 * comments stay pinned); an Unused function inspection; fast inline compiler diagnostics via rlc --grammar-check; a run configuration that compiles and runs with rlc or rlmake (multi-file via *$FROM; auto-detected, incl. rlc.bat in %APPDATA%\Refal-5-lambda on Windows), a run arrow at Go/GO, clickable diagnostics (absolute Windows paths included), helpful guidance when the compiler is missing; a New → Refal File template and live templates; Refal-2 dialect support (content-detected on .ref: dedicated lexer/parser, highlighting incl. /123/ macrodigits and k/name/ … . calls, structure view, brace matching, find usages, case-insensitive navigation) and an Indented asterisk inspection with a move-to-column-0 quick-fix (all dialects); an Unreachable sentences after a catch-all pattern inspection; Refal-2 functions in Navigate → Symbol.

localInspection.displayName: Unreachable sentences after a catch-all pattern

action.description: Create a new Refal-5 Lambda file
